title: More helpful error message on Enum <-> Instantiation name collision
body:
Hi all, 
I've been going through a book which creates a basic microprocessor, and after much debugging, I figured out that it called an instantiation the same name as an enum element.

The current error message hints an internal error, specifically
```
%Error: Internal Error: CPU.v:2:6: ../V3Ast.h:2098: AstNode is not of expected type, but instead has type 'CELL'
    2 |    S s1 (clk);
      |      ^~
                        ... See the manual at https://verilator.org/verilator_doc.html for more assistance.
```
is not clear, and it took me a lot of digging to figure out why it was popping up.

A cut-down reproducible version is this, not sure how else to fit test_regress:
```
module CPU (input logic clk);
   S s1 (clk);
endmodule // CPU

// Sequences operations in a state machine
module S (input clk);
   enum {s0, s1} state;
   always_ff @(clk)
     begin
        $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
        $finish;
     end
endmodule // s
```

`verilator --version` = 5.014

I am using Manjaro Linux.

I wouldn't know where to begin with fixing this, but if you could point me to where I can create more helpful error messages I'll have a crack at fixing this.

Thank you all!
